release_lifecycle.support_statement: Platform preview release. Supported for evaluation and documented theoremOS install flows. The consumer direct-write image is release-blocking and must be proven on Hetzner UEFI hardware before publication.
release_lifecycle.security_support: Best-effort fixes apply only to the latest published preview line. Older preview lines may be superseded without backport commitments.
release_lifecycle.eol_action: When this preview line is superseded or revoked, operators must upgrade to the next supported preview line or re-image using the documented recovery path.

release_channel.name: preview
release_channel.promotion_policy: A preview release may publish only after validate-release.sh, conformance evidence, demo-path evidence, release notes, signed metadata, and governed publication declaration succeed.
release_channel.freeze_policy: Preview release tags freeze contract surfaces and bundled artifacts at tag cut; any fix requires a new tag and a fresh release session.

support_tiers.test_depth: Installer, direct-write Hetzner hardware proof, upgrade-bundle validation, rollback-candidate visibility, and release-contract validation are blocking.
support_tiers.security_servicing: Best-effort security servicing on the latest preview line only.
support_tiers.deprecation_policy: Removal requires an explicit release-contract update and a superseding primary target.

support_tiers.test_depth: Legacy artifact only when explicitly emitted; absence does not block theoremOS release publication.
support_tiers.security_servicing: No independent servicing promise beyond repository hygiene.
support_tiers.deprecation_policy: May be omitted from public releases without blocking the primary theoremOS line.

support_tiers.test_depth: No release obligation until a public artifact ships.
support_tiers.security_servicing: No servicing promise until promoted from Planned.
support_tiers.deprecation_policy: Intent only until a maintained line exists.

support_tiers.test_depth: ESP image build, boot-contract verification, and separate theorem-native validation. Absence does not block FreeBSD release publication.
support_tiers.security_servicing: No independent servicing promise until promoted from Planned.
support_tiers.deprecation_policy: Intent only until a maintained line exists.

security_release_policy.advisory_channel: Security-impacting theoremOS changes are published through release notes, release manifests, and release-integrity metadata in the public release repository.
security_release_policy.release_cadence: The latest preview line receives security fixes as out-of-band release tags when necessary; superseded preview lines receive no backport promise.
security_release_policy.emergency_release_rule: Critical security fixes may bypass feature batching but must still produce signed metadata, release notes, conformance evidence, and revocation-ready publication.
security_release_policy.metadata_key_rotation: Release metadata signing keys must be rotated by publishing a new trust-root file and using the new key for the next signed release session.

release_notes.summary: Patch release for the 0.6.x line: fixes consumer direct-write UEFI boot, first-boot runtime bring-up, and release publication gating.
operator_highlights: Use theoremos-v0.6.3-amd64-direct-write.img.xz for unattended bare-metal deployment on supported Hetzner UEFI hosts.
The release gate now fails closed unless the direct-write artifact hash matches a fresh Hetzner hardware validation report.
Publication is blocked unless the exact shipped direct-write image carries matching Hetzner hardware validation and on-box governed proof.
The direct-write image boots through the installed FreeBSD loader on the ESP, and companion theorem-native UEFI artifacts now ship with a proven two-boot acceptance path.

metadata_authentication.verification_rule: Consumers must verify signed manifest, integrity, release-notes, feed, and revocation metadata against releases/trust-root.json before mirroring or promotion.

upgrade_contract.major_version_boundary_rule: Breaking removals and incompatible semantic changes require a major version boundary.
upgrade_contract.deprecation_window_policy: Machine-consumable surfaces remain available for at least one full minor release after deprecation is published in a manifest.
upgrade_contract.behavior_change_policy: Behavior changes that tighten validation or deny previously accepted input must be classified in the manifest.

paths.state_strategy: preserve theorem/store, theorem/log, and /var/lib/theorem across boot-environment switch
paths.rollback_strategy: activate previous boot environment via beadm/bectl and reboot
paths.operator_guide: Run upgrade.sh from the upgrade-bundle archive on a running 0.5.x system.

paths.state_strategy: ESP partition generation swap — write new ESP image, update UEFI boot variables
paths.rollback_strategy: Restore previous ESP generation from backup partition
paths.operator_guide: Write the ESP image to the target ESP partition and update UEFI NVRAM boot entry.

summary: Theorem 0.6.3 is a patch release on the 0.6.x contract line. It preserves the 0.6.x substrate surfaces while fixing the consumer direct-write theoremOS boot path, generating concrete activation digests, generating first-boot theorem-node secrets, making the direct-write image self-contained by default, and binding publication to exact-artifact Hetzner hardware validation plus on-box governed proof.

new.summary: ZFS dataset lifecycle, content-addressed artifact store, boot environments, snapshot management, and pool governance via theorem-zfs.
FreeBSD jail-based workload isolation with governance admission, RCTL resource limits, and lifecycle management via theorem-compute.
PF firewall rule governance via pf anchors, crossing policy enforcement, and network isolation via theorem-net.
Argon2id+AES-256-GCM secret sealing with Capsicum capability restriction for admin operations via theorem-secrets and theorem-crypto.
MAC veriexec verified execution manifest for all theorem binaries with SHA-256 hashes.
theorem-init PID 1 supervisor with governance runtime, quarantine escalation, service graph, alert dispatch, and boot attestation.
ZFS-backed counterfactual governance lanes with branch engine, timeline graph, merge strategy, and fork ledger via theorem-branch.
Intent compiler, self-challenger, drift detector, circuit breaker, and path-not-taken recording via theorem-auto.
Crossing settlement, privacy budgets, escrow ledger, and data retention via theorem-econ and theorem-settle.
Fleet mesh and distributed compute fabric via theorem-fabric.
Staged rollout image pipeline with governance controls via theorem-image.
Hardware device governance with devfs ruleset management via theorem-devices.
Consequence record ingestion, metabolization, and governed predictive models as first-class substrate objects.
Continuous machine-checkable proof generation for substrate correctness, replacing assertion-based trust.
Governed structural adaptation in response to consequence patterns — the substrate grows specialized organs for consequence domains it encounters.
UEFI-native boot chain: theorem-uefi-loader loads theorem-machine-kernel directly from ESP, no third-party bootloader dependency.

changed.summary: Release publication now fails closed on exact-artifact hardware validation for the consumer direct-write theoremOS image, while continuing to govern FreeBSD and theorem-native surfaces through a single pipeline.

capability_negotiation.surface: releases/feed.json plus releases/v0.6.3/manifest.json, with live runtime discovery at GET /capabilities and GET /capability-negotiation
capability_negotiation.stability_rule: Capability ids published in a 6.x manifest are stable within the 6.x line once released.
consumer_rules: Gate on compatibility_classification before enabling a new capability id.
Treat manifest capability ids and release_integrity metadata as authoritative rather than inferring behavior from prose notes or semver alone.
Use releases/feed.json, releases/v0.6.3/manifest.json, and releases/v0.6.3/integrity.json as the release-time capability source of record for public 6.x releases.
Use GET /capabilities and GET /capability-negotiation to confirm the live runtime discovery surface for a running 6.x node.

semantics: entity_id names the acting or queried entity on public request surfaces.
principal_id names the entity holding authority on authorization surfaces.
granted_by_id names the grantor and must not be interpreted as the principal.
GET /authorizations returns authorizations held by the queried principal entity, not authorizations granted by it.

stable_error_taxonomy.scope: Stable for the published 5.0 discovery, organization, lifecycle, release-verification, and governed-release routes in openapi.yaml.
stable_error_taxonomy.legacy_behavior: Legacy pre-4.0 routes may still return text/plain bodies and are outside this stable taxonomy.

declare_intent.enforcement_rule: Acts must satisfy E4: declared_at plus minimum_intent_execution_interval_ms must be less than or equal to initiated_at.

required_operator_actions: Consume releases/feed.json, releases/v0.6.3/manifest.json, and releases/v0.6.3/integrity.json before mirroring or admitting a 0.6.x release.
Deploy on FreeBSD 14.4-RELEASE or later with ZFS root pool named 'theorem'.
Use theoremos-v0.6.3-amd64-direct-write.img.xz for unattended bare-metal direct disk writes; use theoremos-v0.6.3-x86_64-unknown-freebsd.tar.gz only for installer-driven bare-metal flows.
Install theorem-init to /sbin/theorem-init and remaining binaries to /usr/local/bin/.
Verify the attached hardware validation report matches the exact direct-write artifact hash before promotion or mirroring.

artifacts.name: theoremos-v0.6.3-x86_64-unknown-freebsd-upgrade-bundle.tar.gz
artifacts.state_preservation: preserve theorem/store, theorem/log, and /var/lib/theorem across boot-environment switch

release_integrity.downgrade_prevention_rule: Once a consumer admits the 6.x public feed, it must not mirror or promote a release older than release_integrity.minimum_version without an explicit governed rollback decision.

rollback_authorization.rule: Rollback to an older 6.x artifact requires explicit governed approval before repointing a machine or mirror.
